args:
Boards is a social discussion forum for open communication and community-driven conversations. This beta version of Open Boards, means it’s ready and available for everyone to use. All you need is a registered Gno.land username to start creating threads and commenting anywhere on the open version of Boards.

Don’t have a registered Gno.land username, or are new to Gno.land in general? The quick start guide below can help.

*Gnomes take note: Gno.land and Boards are both under active development, so keep in mind the Boards application is in testing mode, and will only be production ready once Gno.land Beta Mainnet is live. There are many features constantly being added, so your feedback is welcome. Create a thread and let us know!*

### **How To Get a Gno Address and Test GNOT Tokens**

To use Boards, and most Gno.land applications, you’ll need:

1. A Gno address, and
2. Some test GNOT tokens to use on the Gno.land staging network (Boards is currently deployed on Staging)

You can get both in one of two ways:

**Option One: Use the Adena Wallet**

- Download [Adena](https://www.adena.app/)
- Once installed, open the wallet and use the built-in faucet button to receive test GNOT tokens instantly. You’ll want to make sure you are making transactions on the Staging Network.
- Alternatively to the faucet button you could visit [https://faucet.gno.land/](https://faucet.gno.land/) and use your wallet address to receive test GNOT tokens in Staging.

**Option Two: Use the gnokey CLI**

- Set up gnokey on your computer. You can find the instructions on the [Gno.land docs website](https://docs.gno.land/users/interact-with-gnokey).
- Request your test GNOT tokens from the [Faucet Hub](https://faucet.gno.land/) by selecting the Staging Network.

### **How to Register Your Username**

You’ll need to choose and register your Gno.land username on the Staging Network, to do so visit [https://gno.land/r/gnoland/users/v1](https://test11.testnets.gno.land/r/gnoland/users/v1). This username will appear on all your threads, posts and comments on this particular network.

### **How to Start Using Boards**

Visit the [Discussions open board](https://test11.testnets.gno.land/r/gnoland/boards2/v1:OpenDiscussions).

To create a new Board thread:

- Click [Create a Thread](https://test11.testnets.gno.land/r/gnoland/boards2/v1$help&func=CreateThread&boardID=1&body=&title=) within the [Discussions](https://test11.testnets.gno.land/r/gnoland/boards2/v1:OpenDiscussions) board and your Adena Wallet will open automatically, enter the title and body of your post, then approve the transaction.
- If you’re using gnokey, copy the “create thread” command from the ‘actions’ tab with the information, paste it into your terminal, and run the transaction from there.

After your thread is created, you can easily share the link with others so they can join the discussion!

**Why gno.land:**  
In a world consumed by power struggles, misinformation, and a deep lack of transparency, gno.land stands as a beacon of hope. Through its revolutionary smart contract technology and the Gno language, it offers a new vision for the future, where truth is easily discoverable and where decentralized systems can be secure, open, and built on trust. 

**Contributions to gno.land – Past contributions or plans for future contributions:**  
While our direct contributions to gno.land are forthcoming, we are committed to supporting the ecosystem by developing tools that enhance community reach and accessibility. Our future initiatives will focus on creating solutions that empower users and developers, making it easier for the community to engage with and benefit from the gno.land platform.

**Validator Name:** **PRO Delegators**
Validator on enterprise-grade bare-metal servers, monitored 24/7

**Networks**
We running validators in around 15 networks (mainnet, testnet and RPC provider) with 20M AUM:
- ETH
- CosmosHub
- AtomOne
- Nolus
- Osmosis
- Dydx
- Neutron
- Elys
- Namada
- Fetch
- Nillion
- Penumbra
- Nomic
- Gno testnets (almost) 
- ...

**Links to Your Digital Presence:**
- Website: https://pro-delegators.com
- Discord: @rafinoo, @ciskos_nuxianlabs
- eMail: contact@nuxian.ch

**Motivation and contribution:**
We're keen to validate on Gno.land, a platform that embodies an innovative vision of smart contracts. Its approach, focused on simplicity, security, and developer experience, aligns perfectly with our ethos. Choosing Go as our native language marks a technological breakthrough that we want to actively support. Gno.land has the potential to redefine the standards of decentralized development, and we want to be part of this evolution from the start.

args: At NodesHub, we are a professional blockchain infrastructure provider specializing in validator services, RPC endpoints, and IBC relaying. With extensive experience across diverse ecosystems, we deliver robust, secure, and highly available infrastructure for Web3 networks.  Networks You Are Currently Validating: We actively validate on 40+ blockchain networks, including Cosmos-based chains and emerging smart contract platforms. Our infrastructure is optimized for uptime, performance, and rapid recovery.  Full List of Networks: https://nodeshub.online/ Links to Your Digital Presence: •Website: https://nodeshub.online •X\\(Twitter\\): https://x.com/Nodes_Hub •GitHub: https://github.com/NodesHub Contact Details: •Email: contact@nodeshub.online •Telegram: https://t.me/Nodes_Hub •Discord ID: nodeshub Why You Are Interested in Validating on gno.land: We are excited to support Gno.land because of its modular design, developer-first philosophy, and community-centric values all of which align with our mission at NodesHub. Our team shares the long-term vision of building decentralized, composable, and developer-friendly ecosystems, and we’re eager to contribute to Gno.land as it grows.  Contributions to gno.land: - Public RPC Infrastructure: We will deploy and maintain reliable public RPC endpoints to support developers and indexers. - Snapshot Services: Daily snapshot generation for faster node synchronization and chain onboarding - IBC Relayer Support: We plan to enable seamless IBC connections as soon as Gno.land supports IBC integrations - Community Engagement: Active involvement in governance, forums, and contributing to documentation and tooling as needed.

Validator Name: Cogwheel

Cogwgeel is blockchain validator that is validating over 5+ mainnets. The validator has been active since 2023. Cogwheel always tries to contribute to the ecosystem by providing documentations for node deployment, writting blogs and how to guides, provide public endpoints such as REST,gRPC,RPC and WSS on Cosmos SDK based networks. Alongside these services we do provide apps and services that are unique and give users a way of interacting with the blockchain such as the Spectra explorer, Dyson Frontend, Picasso Archive and in the future probably even more apps and services will come online.

Networks Currently Validating Secured Assets Value:

Atom One, Symphony, Juno Network, Nibiru Chain, Elys Network\(Governor\), Voi Network, Dyson Protocol.

Secured Assets Value: At the time of writting around 150K USD.

Digital Presence:

Website: https://cogwheel.zone/

X\(Twitter\): https://x.com/cogwheel_val

GitHub: https://github.com/Cogwheel-Validator

Contact:

E-mail: info@cogwheel.zone

Telegram: https://t.me/nman998

Discord: Kamikaza731 \| Cogwheel 

Why Are We Interested in Validating on Gno.land:

Gnoland provides a unique approach to smart contracts. It is unique and interesting. It would be an great honor to be a part of it.

Contributions to gno.land:

The Spectra Explorer: The current plan is to incorporate Gnoland into the Spectra explorer.

Spectra Gnoland Indexer: Although the explorer is closed source, the indexer that will be used for the data collection is open source under Apache 2.0 at: https://github.com/Cogwheel-Validator/spectra-gnoland-indexer. The indexer also comes with the REST API to give any developer a easy starting point.

When Gnoland incorporates IBC protocol it will be integrated into the Spectra Portal app, links: https://portal.thespectra.io , https://github.com/Cogwheel-Validator/spectra-portal, where user can use to bridge the tokens.

**Validator Name:** ITRocket
ITRocket is a professional validator, infrastructure provider and analytics hub.
**Networks We Are Currently Validating:**
- AtomOne (ATONE)
- Canton (CC)
- Celestia (TIA)
- Lava (LAVA)
- Monad (MON)
- Polkadot (DOT)
- ZetaChain (ZETA)
- 30+ other networks — [full list](https://itrocket.net/delegate/)
**Links to Our Digital Presence:**
- Website: [https://itrocket.net](https://itrocket.net)
- X (Twitter): [https://x.com/itrocket_team](https://x.com/itrocket_team)
- GitHub: [https://github.com/itrocket-team](https://github.com/itrocket-team)
**Contact Details:**
- Email: sam@itrocket.net
- Telegram: [https://t.me/itrocket_team](https://t.me/itrocket_team)
- Discord: itrocket
**Why We're Interested in Validating on gno.land:**
ITRocket is focused on reliable, long-term validator operations and ecosystem support. gno.land’s community-driven approach and architecture align well with how we work across networks. We’d love to contribute by providing stable infrastructure and practical tooling that helps the network grow.
**Contributions & Plans:**
- Participated in testnets
- Provide public endpoints and snapshots
- Deliver monitoring and analytics solutions

### 💡 Why gno.land?
ITRocket is focused on reliable, long-term validator operations and ecosystem support. gno.land's community-driven approach and architecture align well with how we work across networks. We'd love to contribute by providing stable infrastructure and practical tooling that helps the network grow.

- Provide public endpoints and snapshots
- Deliver monitoring and analytics solutions
